response:
The total area of the flower bed and the walkway equals the product of the new length and new width, where the new dimensions are expressed as \( (x + 2w) \) and \( (2x + 2w) \), so the equation is \( (x + 2w)(2x + 2w) \) distribution.  
When the width of the flower bed is 6 feet, substituting this value into the area increase condition yields a quadratic equation that solves to a walkway width of 2 feet distribution.
